questions.q: The Mahalanobis distance is named after which Indian scientist?
questions.explanation: The measure was devised by Prasanta Chandra Mahalanobis, the Indian statistician often called the father of Indian statistics.

questions.q: In which field of study is the Mahalanobis distance primarily used?
questions.explanation: The Mahalanobis distance is a statistical measure used to quantify how far a point lies from a distribution.

questions.q: What does the Mahalanobis distance essentially measure?
options: The speed of a moving particle
How far a point is from the centre of a distribution
questions.explanation: It measures the distance of a point from the mean of a distribution, accounting for the spread and correlations of the data.

questions.q: P. C. Mahalanobis founded which famous Indian institute in 1931?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis founded the Indian Statistical Institute (ISI) in Kolkata in 1931.

questions.q: The Mahalanobis distance is widely applied in which modern discipline?
questions.explanation: It is widely used in data science for tasks such as outlier detection and classification.

questions.q: A key advantage of the Mahalanobis distance over ordinary straight-line distance is that it accounts for the data's:
questions.explanation: Unlike Euclidean distance, the Mahalanobis distance accounts for the correlations and differing scales of the variables.

questions.q: In which country was P. C. Mahalanobis born and did most of his work?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis was born in Calcutta (Kolkata), India, and carried out most of his statistical work there.

questions.q: One common practical use of the Mahalanobis distance is detecting:
questions.explanation: Because it measures how unusual a point is relative to a distribution, it is commonly used to flag outliers.

questions.q: When all variables are uncorrelated and have equal variance, the Mahalanobis distance reduces to which familiar distance?
questions.explanation: With an identity covariance matrix, the Mahalanobis distance simplifies to the ordinary Euclidean distance.

questions.q: The Mahalanobis distance is best described as a measure of what kind of relationship?
options: Between a point and a distribution
questions.explanation: It generalises the idea of distance to describe how far a single point lies from an entire distribution of data.

questions.q: In what year did P. C. Mahalanobis first introduce the distance measure that bears his name?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis introduced the measure in a 1936 paper presented to the National Institute of Sciences of India.

questions.q: The matrix used in the Mahalanobis distance to account for variable spread and correlation is the:
questions.explanation: The Mahalanobis distance uses the inverse of the covariance matrix to weight the differences between coordinates.

questions.q: Mahalanobis originally developed the measure while working on a problem in which field?
questions.explanation: He developed the measure to compare and classify groups using anthropometric (body-measurement) data.

questions.q: If the covariance matrix is the identity, the squared Mahalanobis distance equals:
questions.explanation: With an identity covariance matrix the formula collapses to the ordinary squared Euclidean distance.

questions.q: P. C. Mahalanobis is widely regarded as the founder of which discipline in India?
questions.explanation: He is regarded as the founder of modern statistics in India and pioneered large-scale sample surveys.

questions.q: Besides the distance measure, Mahalanobis pioneered which statistical technique on a large scale in India?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis pioneered large-scale random sample surveys, work that gained him international recognition.

questions.q: The Mahalanobis distance is closely associated with which family of probability distributions when used for classification?
questions.explanation: It arises naturally from the multivariate normal distribution, where contours of equal probability are ellipsoids of constant Mahalanobis distance.

questions.q: National Statistics Day in India is observed on Mahalanobis's birthday, which falls on:
questions.explanation: India observes National Statistics Day on 29 June, the birth anniversary of P. C. Mahalanobis.

questions.q: Which highly influential statistician collaborated with Mahalanobis at the Indian Statistical Institute early in his career?
questions.explanation: C. R. Rao worked at the ISI under Mahalanobis and became one of the most influential statisticians of the 20th century.

questions.q: In machine learning, the Mahalanobis distance is often preferred over Euclidean distance because it is:
options: Faster to compute with no matrix
questions.explanation: By incorporating the covariance structure, it is invariant to scaling and accounts for correlations between features.

questions.q: Mahalanobis played a major role in shaping which aspect of newly independent India?
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis was a key architect of India's Second Five-Year Plan, contributing the influential Mahalanobis model of economic planning.

questions.q: The geometric shape of a set of points at constant Mahalanobis distance from the mean of a multivariate normal distribution is:
questions.explanation: Contours of constant Mahalanobis distance form ellipsoids whose axes are determined by the covariance matrix.

questions.q: For a point x, mean vector mu, and covariance matrix S, the squared Mahalanobis distance is given by:
questions.explanation: The squared Mahalanobis distance is (x - mu) transpose times the inverse covariance matrix times (x - mu).

questions.q: If the data are first standardised to be uncorrelated with unit variance (whitened), the Mahalanobis distance in the original space equals which distance in the transformed space?
questions.explanation: Whitening the data with the inverse covariance makes the Mahalanobis distance equivalent to the Euclidean distance in the transformed coordinates.

questions.q: Under multivariate normality, the squared Mahalanobis distance of points from the mean approximately follows which distribution?
questions.explanation: For multivariate normal data, the squared Mahalanobis distance follows a chi-squared distribution with degrees of freedom equal to the number of variables.

questions.q: The Mahalanobis distance is mathematically central to which classical classification rule?
questions.explanation: Discriminant analysis assigns a point to the class whose distribution gives it the smallest Mahalanobis distance, assuming Gaussian classes.

questions.q: For the Mahalanobis distance to be well defined, the covariance matrix S must be:
questions.explanation: The formula requires the inverse of the covariance matrix, so S must be non-singular (invertible).

questions.q: The journal Sankhya, an important outlet for statistical research, was founded in 1933 by:
questions.explanation: Mahalanobis founded the journal Sankhya: The Indian Journal of Statistics in 1933.

questions.q: An earlier related distance Mahalanobis proposed in the 1920s, used in anthropometry, is sometimes called the:
questions.explanation: His generalised distance, often denoted D-squared, predates and led to the formal Mahalanobis distance of 1936.

questions.q: A robust variant of the Mahalanobis distance for outlier detection typically replaces the sample mean and covariance with estimators that are:
questions.explanation: Robust versions use outlier-resistant estimators of location and scatter (such as the Minimum Covariance Determinant) so that outliers do not distort the estimated distribution.
